forbidden_roots_note: DANNESBURK not used by this script.
next_step: Run the emitted gate list in DOSBox-X/debugger against the source-locked pass162 pose; classify first missing gate instead of trying more coordinates.
non_claims: does not prove the stock original binary reached C080/F0377/F0280
does not claim x=111,y=82 is wrong; it narrows where to instrument before changing coordinates

pass162_context.reason: gated gameplay reached, but source portrait click x=111/y=82 produced no visible candidate transition; blocker is now C007/C080 mouse delivery or front-wall hit-state mismatch before F0280

probe_gates.expect: after x=111,y=82 left click, P0725/P0726 are 111/82, L1109_i_Command == 80, G0432_as_CommandQueue[last].Command == 80 with X=111,Y=82
probe_gates.if_missing: host/DOSBox mouse translation or active mouse-input table is blocking before the original queue
probe_gates.probe: break on F0359 entry and after L1109_i_Command is assigned/written
probe_gates.source: COMMAND.C:1452-1662 F0359_COMMAND_ProcessClick_CPSC (not F0365 in this tree)

probe_gates.if_missing: queue overwrite/drop/BUG0_73 collision or wrong timing before dispatch
probe_gates.probe: break when L1160/L1161/L1162 are loaded from G0432_as_CommandQueue

probe_gates.expect: F0377 is entered; normalized point remains inside C05 wall ornament/portrait hit zone for the source-locked front wall
probe_gates.if_missing: C080 is not dispatched or screen-to-viewport translation is different than the visual click assumption
probe_gates.probe: break on F0377 entry and after PC coordinate normalization

probe_gates.expect: pose map0 x=1 y=3 dir=South touches front square x=1 y=4 opposite face and reaches F0280(sensorData=10)
probe_gates.if_missing: front-wall hit zone/state/sensor face is blocking after F0377 but before F0280
probe_gates.probe: break on F0372 and F0280; log G0306/G0307/G0308, forward square, wall face, sensor type/data

source_audit.point: C007 is the viewport zone used by the PC movement secondary mouse table.
Original command queue storage, first/last indices, queue lock, and pending-click fields.

source_audit.point: Zone-based movement table maps C007_ZONE_VIEWPORT left-click to C080.
Actual mouse-click queue writer: derives command from primary/secondary mouse tables and writes nonzero command plus X/Y into G0432_as_CommandQueue.
Required audit symbol: in this ReDMCSB tree F0365 is turn-party handling, not the C080 mouse queue writer; it is dispatched only for C001/C002 in F0380.
F0380 locks/dequeues command, X, Y from G0432_as_CommandQueue and unlocks before dispatch.
F0380 dispatches only C001/C002 turn commands to F0365.
F0380 dispatches C080 to F0377 with dequeued X/Y.
C080 handler; PC builds normalize screen coordinates by subtracting viewport origin before hit testing.
Empty-hand C05 door-button/wall-ornament hit calls F0372, otherwise object cells call grab/drop paths.
F0372 computes the square in front of the party and invokes F0275 on the wall face opposite party direction.
A clicked champion portrait wall sensor calls F0280 with sensorData/portrait index.

symbol_note: Hard-rule symbol F0365 is audited, but in this ReDMCSB tree F0365 is turn-party; the actual mouse queue writer for C080 is F0359_COMMAND_ProcessClick_CPSC at COMMAND.C:1452-1662.
